research_session_preview.research_question: How should manually supplied research notes become reviewable Perspective candidates without becoming durable knowledge?
research_session_preview.operator_intent: Prepare a candidate-only review bundle from public-safe notes for later human review.
research_session_preview.boundary_notes: Sample preview only; no durable research state, proof, evidence, work item, or perspective promotion is created.

source_reference_previews.title: Public-safe operator notes on review-first research candidates
source_reference_previews.operator_note_summary: Notes describe why source provenance, candidate status, and later human promotion gates should stay separate.
source_reference_previews.boundary_notes: Reference preview only; it is not fetched, enriched, indexed, stored as durable memory, or treated as source of truth.

claim_candidates.claim_text: Manually supplied research material should first appear as candidate review objects before any durable Perspective change.
claim_candidates.boundary_notes: Candidate claim only; it does not create committed state or approval authority.

claim_candidates.claim_text: Future provider or retrieval outputs should remain candidate inputs until a bounded human review path promotes them.
claim_candidates.boundary_notes: Candidate claim only; it does not authorize provider calls, retrieval indexes, or durable writes.

evidence_candidates.evidence_summary: The notes emphasize that candidate review should precede durable Perspective mutation.
evidence_candidates.quality_note: Operator summary requires later source review before promotion.
evidence_candidates.boundary_notes: Evidence candidate only; it is not an Augnes evidence row or proof record.

evidence_candidates.evidence_summary: The notes separate future retrieval and provider lanes from this static review contract.
evidence_candidates.quality_note: Synthetic fixture support; later lane work needs separate validation.
evidence_candidates.boundary_notes: Evidence candidate only; it does not authorize retrieval, provider, DB, or API work.

tension_candidates.summary: A review surface is useful before storage exists, but over-specific preview fields could be mistaken for a durable schema.
tension_candidates.operator_question: Which fields should remain display contracts until a type-only contract is separately added?
tension_candidates.boundary_notes: Tension candidate only; it flags review risk without blocking repo work status or mutating state.

knowledge_gap_candidates.summary: The exact TypeScript response contract and parser behavior have not been designed yet.
knowledge_gap_candidates.why_it_matters: A future UI or route should validate the fixture against a type-only contract before runtime behavior exists.
knowledge_gap_candidates.boundary_notes: Knowledge gap candidate only; it does not create follow-up work automatically.

perspective_delta_candidates.before_summary: Research capability lanes are planned as bounded, non-authoritative candidate inputs.
perspective_delta_candidates.after_summary: The first lane should expose manually supplied source material as candidate review bundles before durable research writes.
perspective_delta_candidates.proposed_update_summary: Refine the research capability lane perspective toward review-first candidate bundles.
perspective_delta_candidates.risk_or_conflict_note: Could be mistaken for a committed Perspective update unless kept candidate-only.
perspective_delta_candidates.boundary_notes: Perspective delta candidate only; no perspective memory is promoted or committed.

follow_up_work_candidates.candidate_summary: Define a TypeScript shape for the static candidate review fixture without adding runtime routes or durable writes.
follow_up_work_candidates.reason: The static fixture should eventually be checked against a type-only response contract before UI rendering is added.
follow_up_work_candidates.boundary_notes: Follow-up candidate only; it does not create a work item, issue, branch, or dispatch.
